24.learning_goal_zh: 帮助学习者识别主句、区分主次信息，理解长句的主干结构。
quick_reminder_zh: 主句是句子的核心，包含完整的主谓结构。
从句、插入语、修饰语是附加信息。
长句的主干 = 谁 + 做了什么 / 是什么。

sections.description_zh: 识别主句、句子主干和核心含义。

questions.instruction_zh: 这句话中，哪一部分是主句？
questions.prompt_en: Although the experiment failed, the researchers learned valuable information.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 "the researchers learned valuable information"，它有完整的主谓结构。"Although the experiment failed" 是从句，起让步作用。
questions.wrong_note_zh: "Although..." 引导的是让步状语从句，不能单独成句。

questions.instruction_zh: 下面哪个选项最能体现这句话的句子主干？
questions.prompt_en: The book, which was published in 2020 and has sold over a million copies, explores climate change.
options: The book has sold over a million copies.
questions.explanation_zh: 主干是主句的核心：The book + explores + climate change。中间是定语从句的补充信息。
questions.wrong_note_zh: 定语从句中的内容（出版时间、销量）是附加细节，不是主干。

questions.instruction_zh: 去掉次要信息，保留核心含义。下列哪一项最接近这句话的核心？
questions.prompt_en: Students who study regularly, unlike those who cram, tend to perform better.
options: Students who study regularly tend to perform better.
Unlike those who cram, students perform.
questions.answer: Students who study regularly tend to perform better.
questions.explanation_zh: 核心信息是：有规律学习的学生表现更好。"unlike those who cram" 是对比说明，可删去但对比组 "who study regularly" 需保留以表达完整含义。
questions.wrong_note_zh: "Students tend to perform better" 过于笼统；"unlike those who cram" 是插入对比，非主干。

questions.instruction_zh: 这句话中的主句是哪一部分？
questions.prompt_en: When the meeting ended, everyone left the room, although a few stayed to ask questions.
options: a few stayed to ask questions
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 "everyone left the room"。"When the meeting ended" 是时间状语从句，"although..." 是让步从句。
questions.wrong_note_zh: 时间状语和让步从句都从属于主句。

questions.instruction_zh: 将长句拆分后，主要信息是：
questions.prompt_en: Despite the rain, the outdoor concert, which had been planned for months, went ahead as scheduled.
options: The concert went ahead as scheduled.
The concert had been planned for months.
questions.answer: The concert went ahead as scheduled.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句核心是 "The concert went ahead as scheduled"。"Despite the rain" 和 "which had been planned for months" 是次要信息。
questions.wrong_note_zh: "had been planned" 是定语从句中的谓语，不是主句动作。

questions.instruction_zh: 下面哪个选项是这句话的句子主干？
questions.prompt_en: In recent years, technology, especially smartphones, has changed how people communicate.
options: In recent years, technology has changed how people communicate.
Technology has changed how people communicate.
Smartphones have changed how people communicate.
questions.answer: Technology has changed how people communicate.
questions.explanation_zh: 主干是 Technology + has changed + how people communicate。"In recent years" 和 "especially smartphones" 是状语和同位语。
questions.wrong_note_zh: "especially smartphones" 是举例说明，不是主语。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择主句
questions.prompt_en: If you finish early, you may leave the room.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 "you may leave the room"，条件从句 "If you finish early" 从属于它。
questions.wrong_note_zh: if 引导的是条件从句，不能单独作主句。

questions.instruction_zh: 去掉次要信息后，保留的是：
questions.prompt_en: The professor, who has won several awards, teaches economics at the university.
options: The professor teaches economics at the university.
The professor has won several awards.
Who has won several awards teaches economics.
questions.answer: The professor teaches economics at the university.
questions.explanation_zh: 去掉定语从句后，主干是 The professor teaches economics at the university。
questions.wrong_note_zh: "has won several awards" 是定语从句内容，可删去。

questions.instruction_zh: 下面哪一项最能体现这句话的核心？
questions.prompt_en: While some prefer coffee, others, including many students, prefer tea for studying.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 "others prefer tea for studying"。"including many students" 是同位语，"While some prefer coffee" 是对比从句。
questions.wrong_note_zh: 主句的主语是 others，谓语是 prefer；"including many students" 只是补充。

questions.instruction_zh: 这句话中的主句是：
questions.prompt_en: The report, which was released yesterday, shows a rise in sales.
options: The report shows a rise in sales.
Which was released yesterday, shows a rise.
questions.answer: The report shows a rise in sales.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 The report shows a rise in sales。定语从句 "which was released yesterday" 提供时间信息。
questions.wrong_note_zh: "was released" 属于定语从句，不是主句谓语。

sections.description_zh: 听句子后选择最能表达句子主干的选项。答题前不显示原文。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: Although the experiment failed, the researchers learned valuable information.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 the researchers learned valuable information。Although 引导让步从句。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: The book, which was published in 2020, explores climate change.
questions.explanation_zh: 主干是 The book explores climate change。which 引导定语从句。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: When the meeting ended, everyone left the room.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 everyone left the room。When 引导时间状语从句。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: Despite the rain, the outdoor concert went ahead as scheduled.
options: the outdoor concert went ahead as scheduled.
questions.answer: the outdoor concert went ahead as scheduled.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 the outdoor concert went ahead as scheduled。Despite the rain 是让步状语。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: Technology, especially smartphones, has changed how people communicate.
options: Technology has changed how people communicate.
questions.answer: Technology has changed how people communicate.
questions.explanation_zh: 主干是 Technology has changed how people communicate。especially smartphones 是同位语。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: The professor, who has won several awards, teaches economics at the university.
options: The professor teaches economics at the university.
The professor has won several awards.
questions.answer: The professor teaches economics at the university.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 The professor teaches economics at the university。定语从句可删去。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: If you finish early, you may leave the room.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 you may leave the room。If 引导条件从句。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: The report, which was released yesterday, shows a rise in sales.
options: The report shows a rise in sales.
questions.answer: The report shows a rise in sales.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 The report shows a rise in sales。定语从句补充时间信息。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: While some prefer coffee, others prefer tea for studying.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是 others prefer tea for studying。While 引导对比从句。

questions.instruction_zh: 请听下面的句子，并选择最能表达句子主干的选项。
questions.prompt_en: In recent years, renewable energy has become more affordable.
options: Renewable energy has become more affordable.
questions.answer: Renewable energy has become more affordable.
questions.explanation_zh: 主干是 Renewable energy has become more affordable。In recent years 是时间状语。

summary_card.body_zh: 长句的主干 = 主句 = 谁 + 做了什么/是什么。从句、插入语、修饰语是附加信息。练习识别主句能帮你快速把握长句核心。

25.learning_goal_zh: 帮助学习者理解从句之间的逻辑关系：因果、对比、条件、时间、目的、结果等。
quick_reminder_zh: 条件：if, unless, as long as

sections.description_zh: 选择最佳连接词，识别从句之间的逻辑关系。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的连接词填空：
questions.prompt_en: _____ the weather was bad, we decided to stay indoors.
questions.explanation_zh: 因果关系：天气不好导致我们决定留在室内。Because 表示原因。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Although 表示让步（尽管），不符合因果逻辑。

questions.instruction_zh: 这两部分之间的逻辑关系是：
questions.prompt_en: She studied hard; therefore, she passed the exam.
questions.explanation_zh: therefore 表示结果：努力学习 → 通过考试，是因果关系。
questions.wrong_note_zh: contrast 表示对比，condition 表示条件，这里没有。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的连接词填空：
questions.prompt_en: _____ you submit the essay by Friday, you will lose points.
questions.explanation_zh: 条件关系：Unless = 如果不。不按时交就会扣分。
questions.wrong_note_zh: If 需搭配 "do not submit" 才能表达相同意思。Unless = if not。

questions.instruction_zh: 用不同的连接词改写原句，保持因果关系。原句：
questions.prompt_en: He was tired, so he went to bed early.
options: Because he was tired, he went to bed early.
Although he was tired, he went to bed early.
When he was tired, he went to bed early.
questions.answer: Because he was tired, he went to bed early.
questions.explanation_zh: so 表结果，because 表原因，二者可互换表达同一因果关系。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Although 会改变含义（尽管累却早睡）；When 强调时间而非因果。

questions.prompt_en: Compare: _____ the two options, "however" is more formal than "but" in academic writing.
questions.explanation_zh: compare + between 表示「比较两者」。Between the two options 是正确搭配。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Whereas/While 用于连接两个从句表示对比，不用于 "compare..." 结构。

questions.instruction_zh: 这句话中主句与从句的逻辑关系是：
questions.prompt_en: The project failed because the team lacked sufficient funding.
questions.explanation_zh: 主句是结果（项目失败），从句是原因（缺乏资金）。because 后接原因。
questions.wrong_note_zh: cause 在 because 之后，effect 在主句。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择表示目的的连接词填空：
questions.prompt_en: She took a taxi _____ she wouldn't be late.
questions.explanation_zh: 目的关系：为了不迟到而打车。so that 表目的。
questions.wrong_note_zh: because 表原因；这里「打车」是手段，「不迟到」是目的。

questions.prompt_en: Choose the better connector for academic writing: "The data suggests a trend. _____, more research is needed."
questions.cue_zh: 选更正式、自然的连接词
questions.explanation_zh: However 比 but 更正式，适合学术写作，表示转折。
questions.wrong_note_zh: But 较口语；So/And 不表示转折。

questions.instruction_zh: 用表示对比/让步的连接词将两句话合并。原句：
questions.prompt_en: It was raining. We still went for a walk.
options: Although it was raining, we still went for a walk.
Because it was raining, we still went for a walk.
When it was raining, we still went for a walk.
questions.answer: Although it was raining, we still went for a walk.
questions.explanation_zh: Although 表示让步：尽管下雨，仍然去散步。体现对比关系。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Because 表因果；When 表时间，都不体现「尽管…仍然」的对比。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择表示时间先后的连接词填空：
questions.prompt_en: _____ the deadline passed, no more submissions were accepted.
questions.explanation_zh: 时间关系：截止日期过后，不再接受提交。After 表时间先后。
questions.wrong_note_zh: 逻辑上是时间先后，而非因果或目的。

summary_card.body_zh: 掌握连接词和从句关系：因果(because/so)、对比(although/however)、条件(if/unless)、时间(when/after)、目的(so that)。TOEFL 写作中准确使用连接词能提升逻辑清晰度。

26.learning_goal_zh: 帮助学习者在 TOEFL 风格的段落中建立句间衔接与连贯。
quick_reminder_zh: 过渡词连接句子，使段落流畅。
First, Second, Furthermore, However, In conclusion 等。
句子顺序影响段落逻辑。

sections.description_zh: 填空选择过渡词，理解段落逻辑与连贯性。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的过渡词填空（两处）：
questions.prompt_en: Online learning has many advantages. _____, it offers flexibility. _____, it can be more affordable.
questions.explanation_zh: 列举优点时用 First... Second... 表示顺序。
questions.wrong_note_zh: However 表转折；In conclusion 用于结尾；这里需要列举过渡。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的过渡词填空：
questions.prompt_en: Some people prefer city life. _____, others enjoy the countryside.
questions.explanation_zh: 前后对比两种偏好，In contrast 表示对比。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Therefore/As a result 表因果；For example 表举例。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的过渡词填空（两处）：
questions.prompt_en: The study had limitations. _____, the sample size was small. _____, more research is needed.
questions.explanation_zh: For instance 举例说明局限；Therefore 引出结论（需要更多研究）。
questions.wrong_note_zh: However 用于转折；这里先举例再结论。

questions.instruction_zh: 下列哪一句最适合接在「One benefit of exercise is improved mood.」之后？
questions.prompt_en: One benefit of exercise is improved mood.
questions.explanation_zh: Furthermore 延续同一论点（运动的好处），添加另一个优点，保持段落连贯。
questions.wrong_note_zh: However 会转折；In conclusion 太早；For example 可接受但 Furthermore 更直接延续「好处」主题。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的过渡词填空：
questions.prompt_en: The experiment showed positive results. _____, we cannot generalize to all populations.
questions.explanation_zh: However 表示转折：尽管有积极结果，但不能泛化。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Furthermore 表示递进；这里需要转折。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句子重新排列成连贯段落。下列哪一句应该放在最后？
options: First, renewable energy reduces carbon emissions.
In conclusion, switching to renewables benefits both the environment and the economy.
Second, it can create new jobs.
Furthermore, costs have decreased in recent years.
questions.answer: In conclusion, switching to renewables benefits both the environment and the economy.
questions.explanation_zh: In conclusion 引导总结句，应放在段落末尾。
questions.wrong_note_zh: First, Second, Furthermore 用于正文；结论句应最后出现。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的过渡词填空（两处）：
questions.prompt_en: Reading improves vocabulary. _____, it expands knowledge. _____, it is a valuable habit.
questions.explanation_zh: Additionally 添加另一好处；Thus 引出总结。
questions.wrong_note_zh: However/In contrast 表转折；这里需要递进和总结。

questions.instruction_zh: "This supports the main argument." 这句话最适合放在哪里？
options: At the start of a paragraph
questions.explanation_zh: "This supports" 指代前文证据，应紧接在证据之后，解释其作用。
questions.wrong_note_zh: 段落开头缺少前文；结论句通常总结全文；不相关想法之间无法「支持」。

questions.prompt_en: Choose the best transition: "Many students struggle with time management. _____, they can improve with practice."
questions.cue_zh: 选择表示「尽管如此/然而可以」的过渡词
questions.explanation_zh: However 表示转折：虽然有困难，但可以通过练习改进。
questions.wrong_note_zh: Therefore 表因果；这里强调转折和希望。

questions.instruction_zh: 选择最合适的过渡词填空（两处）：
questions.prompt_en: _____, let me summarize the key points. _____, education shapes future opportunities.
questions.explanation_zh: In conclusion 引出总结；Ultimately 强调最终要点。
questions.wrong_note_zh: First/Second 用于列举，不适合总结段；For example 不用于结论。

summary_card.body_zh: 过渡词让段落连贯：First/Second 列举，Furthermore 递进，However 转折，For instance 举例，Therefore/Thus 结论，In conclusion 总结。注意句子顺序和逻辑衔接。

27.learning_goal_zh: 帮助学习者将短句合并成更流畅、完整的英语句子。
quick_reminder_zh: 用连接词（so, because, which 等）合并两句话。
用分词结构（After finishing...）连接先后动作。
用 so...that 表达程度与结果。

sections.description_zh: 将下面两句话合并成一句，在输入框中写出合并后的句子。

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: The weather was bad. We stayed home.
questions.answer: The weather was bad, so we stayed home.
acceptable_answers: The weather was bad, so we stayed home.
We stayed home because the weather was bad.
questions.explanation_zh: 用 so 或 because 连接因果，简洁自然。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 so 或 because 连接因果

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: She finished the report. She sent it to her boss.
questions.answer: After finishing the report, she sent it to her boss.
questions.explanation_zh: 分词结构 "After finishing" 使句子更流畅，避免两个短句的机械连接。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 After finishing 的分词结构

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: He studied hard. He passed the exam.
questions.answer: He studied hard, so he passed the exam.
acceptable_answers: He studied hard, so he passed the exam.
He passed the exam because he studied hard.
questions.explanation_zh: 用 so 或 because 连接因果。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 so 或 because 连接因果

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: The book was interesting. I read it in one day.
questions.answer: The book was so interesting that I read it in one day.
questions.explanation_zh: 用 so...that 结构表达程度与结果。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 so...that 表达程度与结果

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: The meeting was cancelled. We had more time to prepare.
questions.answer: The meeting was cancelled, so we had more time to prepare.
acceptable_answers: The meeting was cancelled, so we had more time to prepare.
We had more time to prepare because the meeting was cancelled.
questions.explanation_zh: 用 so 或 because 连接因果。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 so 连接因果

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: Cities are crowded. This can lead to stress and pollution.
questions.answer: Cities are crowded, which can lead to stress and pollution.
questions.explanation_zh: 用 which 引导定语从句拓展，说明「拥挤」的后果。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 which 引导定语从句

questions.instruction_zh: 将下面两句话合并为一句。
questions.prompt_en: Online learning is popular. It offers flexibility.
questions.answer: Online learning is popular because it offers flexibility.
questions.explanation_zh: 用 because 从句说明原因。
questions.hint_zh: 试试用 because 从句说明原因

summary_card.body_zh: 合并短句：用 so/because 连接因果；用 After finishing 等分词结构连接先后动作；用 so...that 表达程度与结果；用 which 引导定语从句拓展。

28.learning_goal_zh: 帮助学习者在写作和口语中避免重复简单词，选用更自然、正式的同义表达。
quick_reminder_zh: 用 beneficial / harmful、crucial / significant 等替换 good / bad / important。
用 in order to 表目的；用 numerous、extremely 等减少 very / many 的重复。
改写句子时保持原意，提升词汇多样性与正式度。

sections.description_zh: 将句中的简单词替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。答题时忽略大小写与标点。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 good 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Regular exercise is good for mental health.
questions.explanation_zh: beneficial 表示「有益的」，比 good 更准确、正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 to（表目的）替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The government introduced the policy to reduce pollution.
questions.explanation_zh: 表目的时 in order to 比 to 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 bad 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Smoking is bad for your health.
questions.explanation_zh: harmful 明确表示「有害的」，比 bad 更准确。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 very 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The results were very significant.
questions.explanation_zh: extremely / highly 比 very 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 many 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Many students participated in the survey.
questions.explanation_zh: numerous 或 a large number of 比 many 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 important 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: It is important to protect the environment.
questions.explanation_zh: crucial 表示「至关重要的」，比 important 更有力度。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 got 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: She got a scholarship to study abroad.
questions.explanation_zh: obtained / received 表示「获得」，比 got 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 think 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Researchers think that the new method is more effective.
questions.explanation_zh: 学术语境中 believe / argue 比 think 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 important 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Time management is important for academic success.
questions.explanation_zh: crucial 强调「关键性的」。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 big 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The study found a big difference between the two groups.
questions.explanation_zh: significant 在学术写作中常表示「显著的（差异/影响）」。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 good 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The experiment produced good results.
questions.explanation_zh: positive results 是学术写作中的常见搭配。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 shows 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The report shows that the situation is deteriorating.
questions.explanation_zh: indicates / demonstrates 比 shows 稍正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 get 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: People use the internet to get information on a daily basis.
questions.explanation_zh: obtain 比 get 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 shows 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The data shows a clear trend.
questions.explanation_zh: suggests / indicates 比 shows 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将 do something 替换为更正式的表达（两个词），在输入框写出。
questions.prompt_en: We need to do something to address this issue.
questions.explanation_zh: take action 比 do something 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 very bad 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: The problem is very bad and affects numerous people.
questions.explanation_zh: serious 替换 very bad，更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 give 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Schools give students the skills they need.
questions.explanation_zh: provide 比 give 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 tried 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Researchers tried to find a solution.
questions.explanation_zh: attempted 比 tried 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 enough 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Getting enough sleep is crucial for students.
questions.explanation_zh: sufficient / adequate 比 enough 更正式。

questions.instruction_zh: 将句中的 changed 替换为更正式的表达，在输入框写出替换词。
questions.prompt_en: Technology has changed how people communicate.
questions.explanation_zh: transformed 比 changed 更有力度、更正式。

summary_card.body_zh: 用 beneficial / harmful、crucial / significant、in order to、numerous、extremely 等替换简单词；答题时忽略大小写与标点，重在写出替换词。
